opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I didn't even know it was there. My daughter saw blood on my sock and checked my foot. It doesn't hurt at all.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My daughter found a deep, smelly wound on the sole of my foot that I can't feel.

symptom_narrative.onset: found today; unknown how long present
symptom_narrative.character: deep painless hole on the sole with smelly weeping fluid
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: sole of the right foot under the ball/big-toe area
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: walking on it (but can't feel it)
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: malodorous discharge, blood on the sock; no fever/chills
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: foot deformity and numbness for years
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: walks around, often barefoot; can't sense injury

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: type 2 diabetes for ~15 years, not well controlled
past_medical_history.how_managed: metformin and insulin, takes them irregularly

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: numb feet (diabetic nerve damage) and foot deformity

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: walks barefoot indoors; poor glycaemic control

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: not perceiving the wound as serious because it is painless

objective_data_offered.value: a 2 cm deep hole on the sole under the big-toe joint, ringed by thick hard callus, with slough and smelly weeping fluid; a little surrounding redness
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: daughter's description/camera; cannot probe depth or assess for bone involvement
afebrile; single reading (fever may be blunted in diabetes)
